leadText: A Tera Orb (Japanese: テラスタルオーブ Terastal Orb) is a Key Item introduced in Generation IX. It is a Poké Ball-shaped item that allows a Trainer's Pokémon to Terastallize.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Allows the player 's Pokémon to Terastallize in battle . After being used, it must be recharged at a Pokémon Center or an unvisited Tera Raid crystal to be used again, except after the first portion of a Titan Pokémon battle , between Pokémon League battles, and after each battle inside Area Zero . During Tera Raid Battles, it starts uncharged regardless of its previous state and requires three damaging moves to be used in order to charge it. However, it can still only be used once per battle. Its current state is not altered after the battle. During the battle with Stellar Form Terapagos at the end of The Indigo Disk DLC, it works similarly, but it only requires two damaging moves and can be used up to four times in the battle. After that battle, the orb becomes overcharged with Terapagos's Terastal energy and no longer needs to be recharged between battles.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V An orb that holds within it the power to crystallize. When it is charged with energy, it can be used to cause Pokémon to Terastallize.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 History 1.3 Effect 1.4 Description 1.5 Acquisition In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price S V N/A N/A History [ edit | edit source ] 10 years prior to the events of Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , while in Area Zero , Professor Sada S / Professor Turo V began investigating the Terastal phenomenon , and discovered that the energy emitted from shining crystals in Area Zero is what triggers it. The Tera Orbs were then created by the professor from the Terastal crystals located in Area Zero, with support from director Clavell . This allowed people to Terastallize their Pokémon outside of Area Zero. The Tera Orb was then shared to the Paldea League and Naranja Academy S / Uva Academy V . Effect [ edit | edit source ] Allows the player 's Pokémon to Terastallize in battle . After being used, it must be recharged at a Pokémon Center or an unvisited Tera Raid crystal to be used again, except after the first portion of a Titan Pokémon battle , between Pokémon League battles, and after each battle inside Area Zero . During Tera Raid Battles, it starts uncharged regardless of its previous state and requires three damaging moves to be used in order to charge it. However, it can still only be used once per battle. Its current state is not altered after the battle. During the battle with Stellar Form Terapagos at the end of The Indigo Disk DLC, it works similarly, but it only requires two damaging moves and can be used up to four times in the battle. After that battle, the orb becomes overcharged with Terapagos's Terastal energy and no longer needs to be recharged between battles. Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V An orb that holds within it the power to crystallize. When it is charged with energy, it can be used to cause Pokémon to Terastallize. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method S V Mesagoza (from Nemona after defeating a Team Star Grunt harassing Penny )

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The crystals found within Tera Orbs bear a striking resemblance to Terapagos in its dormant form.
